artifacts.title: Studio visits are still by appointment only
artifacts.summary: We do not keep open retail or drop-in hours, so please reach out before heading over.
routingTerms: studio visits are still by appointment only
we do not keep open retail or drop-in hours, so please reach out before heading over.

artifacts.title: Public studio updates are now live
artifacts.summary: Non-member announcements now have a public home while member-only logistics stay inside the portal.

non-member announcements now have a public home while member-only logistics stay inside the portal.

artifacts.title: Best way to reach the studio this week
artifacts.summary: Email and the support form are the fastest path for new requests, scheduling questions, and fit checks.

artifacts.title: Website public announcements feed
artifacts.summary: Current public announcements used for broad policy reminders and studio-wide updates.

artifacts.title: Accessibility statement
artifacts.summary: We support accessible access, invite requests for reasonable accommodations, and provide interim alternatives when immediate change is unavailable.
routingTerms: we support accessible access, invite requests for reasonable accommodations, and provide interim alternatives when immediate change is unavailable.

artifacts.title: Clay & materials policy
artifacts.summary: Use approved clay bodies and glazes, disclose the full material stack, and route first-use or unknown materials to review before loading.
routingTerms: use approved clay bodies and glazes, disclose the full material stack, and route first-use or unknown materials to review before loading.

artifacts.title: Community conduct
artifacts.summary: Respect, safety, and shared-space etiquette are required across studio and support channels, and interim safety steps may be used while conduct review is underway.
routingTerms: respect, safety, and shared-space etiquette are required across studio and support channels, and interim safety steps may be used while conduct review is underway.

artifacts.title: Damage & responsibility
artifacts.summary: Firing carries process variation. Damage reports are acknowledged with a documented review path, and confirmed studio-side mistakes default to generous firing-service credits after human review.
routingTerms: firing carries process variation. damage reports are acknowledged with a documented review path, and confirmed studio-side mistakes default to generous firing-service credits after human review.

artifacts.title: Firing & scheduling policy
artifacts.summary: Firing dates are estimate bands, deadline requests require staff confirmation, pre-load changes are no-penalty, and staged or loaded changes are best-effort only.
routingTerms: firing dates are estimate bands, deadline requests require staff confirmation, pre-load changes are no-penalty, and staged or loaded changes are best-effort only.

artifacts.title: Media accessibility standard
artifacts.summary: Public speech media should include captions, long media should include transcript access, and critical accessibility gaps should delay release or require an accessible alternative.
routingTerms: public speech media should include captions, long media should include transcript access, and critical accessibility gaps should delay release or require an accessible alternative.

artifacts.title: Payment, refunds, and cancellations
artifacts.summary: Firing services are charged when work is accepted into service, unpaid cancellations before acceptance are penalty-free, and confirmed studio-side firing mistakes are resolved with generous credits after review.
routingTerms: firing services are charged when work is accepted into service, unpaid cancellations before acceptance are penalty-free, and confirmed studio-side firing mistakes are resolved with generous credits after review.

artifacts.title: Safety & kiln rules
artifacts.summary: Follow posted kiln safety guidance at all times. Use wet cleanup or approved dust controls, required PPE, and fully label all kiln-bound work.
routingTerms: follow posted kiln safety guidance at all times. use wet cleanup or approved dust controls, required ppe, and fully label all kiln-bound work.

artifacts.title: Storage limits & abandoned work
artifacts.summary: Pickup-ready work gets 2.75 weeks of grace by default, optional prepaid storage before billed storage starts, billed storage at $1.50 per half-shelf per day, and studio reclamation after 28 billed days.
routingTerms: pickup-ready work gets 2.75 weeks of grace by default, optional prepaid storage before billed storage starts, billed storage at $1.50 per half-shelf per day, and studio reclamation after 28 billed days.

artifacts.title: Studio access & supervision
artifacts.summary: Studio visits are appointment-only, reservations are required for all visits, and access details are shared only after account verification and approved booking context.
routingTerms: studio visits are appointment-only, reservations are required for all visits, and access details are shared only after account verification and approved booking context.

artifacts.title: API support submission workflow
artifacts.summary: API v1 support request route used by agent or structured support workflows.

artifacts.title: Docs policy index
artifacts.summary: Generated index of canonical policy documents and their machine-readable agent metadata.

artifacts.title: Where can I find billing receipts?
artifacts.summary: Go to Billing in the portal for recent charges and invoice details. If you need a specific receipt, submit a support request.

artifacts.title: How do I keep a copy of my reservation history?
artifacts.summary: Open Support and request a continuity export if you need a full JSON or CSV archive.

artifacts.title: How can I check kiln timing?
artifacts.summary: Open Kiln Schedule and your piece timeline in the portal for the latest estimate. Timing can shift based on queue and load changes.

artifacts.title: Can I change my membership plan?
artifacts.summary: Open Membership in the portal to review your current tier and available options, then submit a change request.

artifacts.title: How do pickup and drop-off work?
artifacts.summary: Use your portal timeline for status updates. When your pieces are marked ready, you can pick up during normal studio hours.

artifacts.title: When should I submit a support request?
artifacts.summary: Use support requests for non-urgent studio questions and appointment-planning questions. For urgent issues, email support directly so we can respond faster.

artifacts.title: I need billing help or a receipt. What should I do?
artifacts.summary: Submit a support request with the date, amount, and what you need.

artifacts.title: Can I reserve kiln time from the portal?
artifacts.summary: Use the form to request a booking or change, and include your clay body, size, and preferred window.

artifacts.title: How do kiln firings get scheduled?
artifacts.summary: Firings are batched by clay body and kiln availability. Timing can shift.

artifacts.title: How do I change, pause, or cancel my membership?
artifacts.summary: Send a request with your current plan and the change you want, and we will handle it.

artifacts.title: When will my pieces be ready for pickup?
artifacts.summary: We post updates as each firing completes. Check My Pieces for the latest status and timeline.

artifacts.title: What is the shelf-space and storage policy?
artifacts.summary: Finished work enters a pickup-ready timeline as soon as it is marked ready. Coordinate prepaid storage before billed storage begins.

artifacts.summary: Recommended faqItems seed data and supportRequests schema documentation.

artifacts.summary: Fallback support answers used when live faqItems cannot be loaded.

artifacts.title: Portal support submission workflow
artifacts.summary: Portal support form write path used for non-urgent support requests.

artifacts.title: Website public FAQ feed
artifacts.summary: Public FAQ entries surfaced on the website for studio, kiln, billing, and access questions.

artifacts.title: Can you pick up supplies for me?
artifacts.summary: Yes. Members can request supplies through the portal and pick them up during a reservation, or ask for delivery.
routingTerms: can you pick up supplies for me?

artifacts.title: Do you load the kiln for me?
artifacts.summary: Yes. Kilns are loaded by studio technicians so spacing, supports, and kiln furniture are handled safely and consistently.
routingTerms: do you load the kiln for me?

artifacts.title: Do you offer credits if there is a firing mistake?
artifacts.summary: Yes. If review shows the issue was studio-side, we apply generous credits to the affected firing service. Maker-side issues like clay, construction, glaze behavior, or firing-direction problems are not automatically credited.
routingTerms: do you offer credits if there is a firing mistake?

artifacts.title: Do you offer reduction firing?
artifacts.summary: Not yet. A raku reduction kiln is planned for the property, and we will announce it through the portal when it is ready.

artifacts.title: How do I estimate how many half shelves I need?
artifacts.summary: If your work fits laid flat inside a carry-on, that is about one half shelf. Tall pieces need clearance; handles and irregular shapes take extra room; stacks (like plates) are more efficient. When in doubt, ask and we will help size it.
routingTerms: how do i estimate how many half shelves i need?

artifacts.title: How do I get the address and gate code?
artifacts.summary: We share the exact address and access details through the portal once your account is verified and you have a confirmed reservation, day pass, or approved visit context. Create a free portal account here .
routingTerms: how do i get the address and gate code?

artifacts.title: How do I submit a firing request?
artifacts.summary: Submit a firing request through the portal with your clay body, glaze notes, firing type, and any deadline you need reviewed. You will see the current scheduling estimate band there too.
routingTerms: how do i submit a firing request?

artifacts.title: How do memberships work?
artifacts.summary: Membership tiers include A la Carte, Apprentice, Journeyman, and Master, with varying firing credits, storage, and discounts. Compare tiers on the Memberships page; pricing is published in the portal. We are happy to help you choose.

artifacts.title: How do payments work?
artifacts.summary: Firing services are charged when work is accepted into service. Supplies and other online payments still run through Stripe, and in-person payments can be handled by tap-to-pay POS. If a studio-side firing mistake is confirmed, we apply generous credits after review.

artifacts.title: How is Potter of the Month selected?
artifacts.summary: Potter of the Month features are selected from active studio members. We look at the work moving through the kilns and rotate highlights through the membership. It is a member-only feature rather than an open nomination.
routingTerms: how is potter of the month selected?

artifacts.title: How often are workshops offered?
artifacts.summary: Workshops are occasional and announced when the studio schedule allows. Invites go to members and details are posted in the portal.

artifacts.title: How often do you fire?
artifacts.summary: We generally fire at least once per week and add extra loads when demand is high, but posted firing windows are estimate bands rather than guarantees. Most weeks include both bisque and mid-fire glaze firings.

artifacts.title: What are the kiln firing prices?
artifacts.summary: Half shelf = about a carry-on suitcase laid flat. Tall pieces need clearance; handles waste space; stacks are more efficient. If you are unsure, send a quick photo and we will help estimate. We bill by per-shelf or whole-kiln only; we do not bill by kiln volume. Full Kiln (or custom fire) — $60 Half-Shelf Bisque — $15 Half-Shelf Glaze — $25 Community Shelf — $0 (lowest priority, space permitting, with shelf-owner permission, and excluded from firing triggers) Delivery — $25 per trip
routingTerms: what are the kiln firing prices?

artifacts.title: What is a day pass?
artifacts.summary: Day passes are $40 and include appointment-only access to studio equipment and work areas by reservation. Book through the portal so we can confirm your visit window before sharing access details.

artifacts.title: What is Monsoon Fire Pottery?
artifacts.summary: Monsoon Fire is a production-first ceramics studio in the West Valley. We work out of a 2,500 sqft basement studio plus 1,000 sqft of garage and outdoor workspace, built for steady firing, quiet making, and dependable support.

artifacts.title: What kiln do you use?
artifacts.summary: We fire in an L&L eQ2827-3 electric oxidation kiln with a Genesis touch screen controller, rated to cone 10. A raku reduction kiln is planned for the property.

artifacts.title: Who is the studio for?
artifacts.summary: We are most useful for production potters and serious hobbyists who need dependable firing and workspace. If you are brand-new to clay and want structured group workshops, we can point you to great Phoenix studios. If you want focused, independent time, you will feel at home here.

artifacts.title: Website policy summaries
artifacts.summary: Public website policy-summary feed generated from the canonical docs set.

artifacts.title: Firing-damage notice and follow-up
artifacts.summary: Kiln Fire customer notices acknowledge firing issues, document the affected piece, and follow up with next steps.
artifacts.observedPractice: The customer-facing path acknowledges the incident quickly and follows up after staff review.
artifacts.canonicalConcern: The policy now requires documented acknowledgment and a review path for customer-facing damage reports while keeping remedies human-reviewed and non-promissory.

artifacts.title: Mistaken membership charge followed by refund
artifacts.summary: Customer-facing support verified a mistaken membership charge and confirmed a refund after review.
routingTerms: mistaken membership charge followed by refund
artifacts.observedPractice: Support corrected the mistaken membership charge and issued a refund after verifying the error.
artifacts.canonicalConcern: Policy now explicitly covers full refunds for paid mistaken charges, no-penalty unpaid cancellations, and prorated review after work starts.

artifacts.title: Pickup and storage timing reminders
artifacts.summary: Reminder sequences and pickup coordination are already part of customer-facing Kiln Fire communication.
artifacts.observedPractice: Support sends reminder-style communication before storage deadlines and coordinates pickup timing.
artifacts.canonicalConcern: Current storage policy broadly aligns with the reminder behavior and fee timeline.

artifacts.title: Reservation cleanup and reschedule behavior
artifacts.summary: Customer conversations show manual cleanup and reschedule coordination when reservation plans change.
artifacts.observedPractice: Support coordinates revised dates and cleanup behavior manually instead of promising automatic schedule changes.
artifacts.canonicalConcern: Policy now states that pre-load changes are no-penalty, staged or loaded changes are best-effort only, and deadline requests require staff confirmation.
